strengths: 15+ years fullstack experience with recent TypeScript/Node.js product work at re:cinq and Optilyz, exceeding the 6+ year requirement
Hands-on AI agent development: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks — a rare, explicitly required skill
Deep testing culture: TDD/pairing/trunk-based at RIDE, authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), taught testing courses
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere on the resume, despite it being in the primary stack
Frontend history leans heavily on Ember.js; React appears only once (Optilyz), so React depth is unclear
No Kotlin and no Python; Java exposure is dated (robotics work at Altom, 2016-2018)

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js and thin React evidence

response.justification: The candidate meets or exceeds nearly every core competency: 6+ years fullstack (15+ actual, TypeScript/Node at re:cinq and Optilyz), databases (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL), cloud and containers (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes for GISCollective), Git, and a CS bachelor's degree. The explicit AI-agent requirement is directly evidenced by MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq. Testing expertise is exceptional — TDD practice at RIDE plus authored test tooling — though Vitest/Jest are not named. The main evidence gaps are Nest.js (absent), limited documented React work (one role), and no Kotlin/Python. These are framework-level gaps a polyglot engineer (D, C++, Java, Swift, PHP) with demonstrated fast tooling adoption should close quickly, but they prevent a top score.
